---
title: "Fish-Bird Predation Model Everglades"
subtitle: "Weecology - University of Florida"
author: "A. Blochel"
format: pdf
---

# Model Equations

## Small Fish Dynamics

$$
\frac{dS}{dt} = r_s S \left(1 - \frac{S}{K_s}\right) - b \cdot \frac{50}{W} \cdot B(t,S) \cdot S - a S L
$$

Where:

- $S$ = small fish density
- $r_s$ = intrinsic growth rate of small fish
- $K_s$ = carrying capacity for small fish
- $b$ = bird predation base rate
- $W$ = water depth (cm)
- $B(t,S)$ = bird abundance (function of time and small fish density)
- $a$ = large fish predation rate on small fish
- $L$ = large fish density

**Terms:**

- First term: logistic growth of small fish
- Second term: bird predation (increases with concentration $50/W$ and bird abundance)
- Third term: large fish predation 

## Large Fish Dynamics

$$
\frac{dL}{dt} = \begin{cases} 
c a S L - d L - e_{max} e^{-e_{shape}(W-10)} L & \text{if } W \geq 10 \\
-L & \text{if } W < 10
\end{cases}
$$

Where:

- $L$ = large fish density
- $c$ = conversion efficiency (small fish biomass to large fish growth)
- $a$ = predation rate of large fish on small fish
- $S$ = small fish density
- $d$ = natural mortality rate of large fish
- $e_{max}$ = maximum emigration rate
- $e_{shape}$ = shape parameter for emigration response
- $W$ = water depth (cm)

**Terms:**

- First term: growth from consuming small fish
- Second term: natural mortality
- Third term: emigration (increases exponentially as depth approaches 10 cm threshold)
- Complete removal when water depth falls below 10 cm

## Bird Abundance

$$
B(t,S) = B_{water}(t) + \frac{b_{response} \cdot S}{h_{sat} + S}
$$

where:

$$
B_{water}(t) = \begin{cases}
10 & \text{if } W > 40 \\
10 + 90 \cdot \frac{40 - W}{40} & \text{if } W \leq 40
\end{cases}
$$

Where:

- $B(t,S)$ = total bird abundance (depends on water depth and small fish)
- $B_{water}(t)$ = bird response to water depth
- $b_{response}$ = maximum additional birds attracted by fish availability
- $h_{sat}$ = half-saturation constant for bird numerical response to fish
- $S$ = small fish density
- $W$ = water depth (cm)

**Mechanism:**

- Birds arrive when water becomes shallow ($W \leq 40$ cm)
- Additional birds are attracted by high small fish density (Type II functional response)

## Water Depth

$$
W(t) = 30 + 25 \sin\left(\frac{2\pi t}{365}\right)
$$

Where:

- $W(t)$ = water depth at time $t$ (cm)
- $t$ = time (days)
- Mean depth = 30 cm
- Amplitude = 25 cm (depth ranges from 5 to 55 cm)
- Period = 365 days (annual cycle)
